g.text: TCP Socket Lifecycle — socket() → bind() → listen() → accept() → close()
KEY INSIGHT: Two Distinct File Descriptors
EADDRINUSE | bind() fails | SO_REUSEADDR not set — TIME_WAIT occupies port
EMFILE | accept() fails | Per-process FD limit (default 1024) exceeded
ENFILE | accept() fails | System-wide FD table full
ECONNRESET | recv() = -1 | Client sent RST — treat as EOF, close fd
EPIPE | send() = -1 | Client closed before response — MSG_NOSIGNAL
EINTR | any syscall = -1 | Signal interrupted — retry the call
EMFILE recovery: usleep(100ms) + continue — connections closing free slots
Filled by NIC interrupt / TCP stack
fd[3] | server_fd| sock: LISTENING ← never read/write
fd[4] | client_fd| sock: CONNECTED ← recv()/send() here
fd[5] | client_fd| sock: CONNECTED ← next accept()
socket() | returns fd>=0 or -1 | ~300 ns | creates kernel sock struct
bind() | returns 0 or -1 | ~200 ns | claims addr:port in kernel
listen() | returns 0 or -1 | ~100 ns | creates SYN+accept queues
accept() | returns fd>=0 or -1 | BLOCKS | dequeues from accept queue
recv() | returns n, 0, or -1 | BLOCKS | copies kernel buf → user buf
send() | returns n or -1 | MAY BLK | copies user buf → kernel buf
close() | returns 0 or -1 | ~100 ns | decrements fd refcount
accept() errno EINTR → retry | EMFILE → FD limit reached
